title: pilea approximata var. approximata
intro: Pilea approximata var. approximata, also known as the aluminum plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to Madagascar. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pilea approximata var. approximata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving respiratory function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be made into a tea or infused in oil to extract its medicinal properties, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
